name: SCD Type 2 (Preserve History)
description: Implement SCD Type 2: close old records and insert new versions when attributes change

checks.name: Total 5 rows in dim_customer_scd2
checks.query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM dim_customer_scd2;
checks.description: 3 original + 1 new C001 version + 1 new C004 = 5 (old C001 closed, C002 unchanged, C003 untouched)

checks.name: C001 has 2 versions
checks.query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM dim_customer_scd2 WHERE customer_id = 'C001';
checks.description: C001 changed — old record closed, new record inserted

checks.name: Current C001 is in Campinas, Premium
checks.query: SELECT city, segment FROM dim_customer_scd2 WHERE customer_id = 'C001' AND is_current = true;
checks.description: The new (current) version of C001 should reflect city=Campinas, segment=Premium

checks.name: Old C001 is closed
checks.query: SELECT valid_to, is_current FROM dim_customer_scd2 WHERE customer_id = 'C001' AND city = 'São Paulo';
checks.description: The old C001 record must have valid_to = '2025-01-31' and is_current = false

checks.name: C002 unchanged — still 1 row
checks.query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM dim_customer_scd2 WHERE customer_id = 'C002';
checks.description: C002 had no changes — no new version should be inserted

checks.name: C004 inserted as current
checks.query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM dim_customer_scd2 WHERE customer_id = 'C004' AND is_current = true;
checks.description: New customer C004 must be inserted with is_current = true
